title: ericameria nauseosa var. glabrata
intro: Ericameria nauseosa var. glabrata, also known as gray rabbitbrush, is a shrub-like perennial plant belonging to the daisy family Asteraceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties, which are derived from its essential oils, including camphor and borneol. The gray rabbitbrush has been used to treat a range of health issues, including sore throats, coughs, and respiratory infections, as well as skin irritations and wounds. Gray rabbitbrush can be prepared into herbal teas, salves, and infusions to harness its medicinal benefits, and it is also used in traditional folk medicine practices in the western United States.
